title: draba densifolia f. nelsonii
intro: Draba densifolia f. nelsonii is a subspecies of the Draba densifolia plant, a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States, specifically in the states of California, Oregon, and Washington. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Draba densifolia f. nelsonii typically involve infusions, teas, or tinctures made from the plant's leaves, stems, and roots, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and respiratory problems.
